title: Pentameris basutorum
intro: Pentameris basutorum, also known as Basutoland grass or Pentameris, is a type of grass belonging to the Poaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pentameris basutorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pentameris basutorum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of dried leaves, powder, or essential oil.
taxonomy: Pentameris basutorum is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Meliceae, which comprises various grass species. Within the tribe, Pentameris basutorum is further classified as the genus Pentameris, a group of small to medium-sized grasses.
